title: Option (c) coder-in-VM memory feasibility — 30B + a 6 GB toolchain VM co-residence
purpose: Answer the load-bearing memory question for security Decision 1's hybrid direction: does the cross-platform coder executing inside the network-less Alpine VM fit the 31.323 GiB ceiling alongside the 30B coder model?

hardware.ram: 32 GB LPDDR5X (31.323 GiB effective, one shared pool)

method: self-restoring: stop AO (lean) -> start-llm coder-30b -Force -> measure In-Use -> add a STATIC 6 GB Hyper-V VM (toolchain-guest proxy; host reserves the 6 GB) -> measure peak In-Use -> reset VM config + stop 30B + restart AO. ~2 GiB coordinator dev-session overhead present (named).

measurements.lean_box_adjustment_note: subtract ~2 GiB coordinator-session overhead -> ~5.8 GiB headroom with the 6 GB VM

verdict: Option (c) is MEMORY-FEASIBLE: the 30B + a 6 GB coder-toolchain VM co-reside at 27.5 GiB, under the 31.323 GiB ceiling, with headroom. The RAM concern that gated the Decision-1 hybrid direction is answered.
not_measured: guest-vs-host code-execution SPEED (needs a vsock-driven build in the guest — separate harness)
the VM's ACTUAL toolchain memory use under a real build (static 6 GB is an upper-bound reservation proxy, conservative)
cross_reference: #777 (30B footprint ~15 GiB, consistent); Decision 1 (#787 c.1574); PERFORMANCE_LOG.md 2026-07-10
